description: Build a complete task management REST API with CRUD, priority, assignment, filtering, pagination, validation, and API key auth

stories.title: CRUD endpoints for tasks
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/tasks with { title } returns 201 with { data: { id, title, description, status, created_at, updated_at } } where status defaults to 'todo' and description defaults to ''
GET /api/tasks returns 200 with { data: [...] } containing all tasks; GET /api/tasks/:id returns 200 with { data: {...} } for existing task or 404 with { error: string } for non-existent id

PUT /api/tasks/:id updates provided fields (title, description, status) and returns 200 with { data: {...} } including updated updated_at; returns 404 for non-existent id; returns 400 if status is not one of 'todo', 'in_progress', 'done'
DELETE /api/tasks/:id returns 204 with no body for existing task, 404 with { error: string } for non-existent id
POST /api/tasks with missing or empty title returns 400 with { error: string }
Tests in tests/tasks.test.ts cover all CRUD operations including 201, 200, 204, 400, and 404 responses

stories.title: Priority field with enum constraint
acceptanceCriteria: Tasks table has a 'priority' column accepting values: 'low', 'medium', 'high', 'urgent' with default 'medium'
POST /api/tasks without priority field creates task with priority 'medium'; POST with explicit priority persists it
PUT /api/tasks/:id accepts priority field and persists the change; returns updated task with new priority
POST or PUT with priority value not in ('low', 'medium', 'high', 'urgent') returns 400 with { error: string }
Tests in tests/priority.test.ts verify default priority, explicit setting, update, and invalid value rejection

stories.title: Assignee field with user tracking
acceptanceCriteria: Tasks table has 'assigned_to' column (nullable string); POST and PUT /api/tasks accept assigned_to field; GET endpoints include assigned_to in response
POST /api/tasks/:id/assign with { assigned_to: string } updates the task and returns 200 with { data: {...} }; returns 404 if task does not exist
POST /api/tasks/:id/unassign sets assigned_to to null and returns 200 with { data: {...} }; returns 404 if task does not exist
Tests in tests/assignee.test.ts verify assignment via POST/PUT, dedicated assign/unassign endpoints, 404 on non-existent task, and that assigned_to appears in GET responses

stories.title: Filtering tasks by status, priority, and assignee
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/tasks?status=todo returns only tasks matching that status value
GET /api/tasks?priority=high returns only tasks matching that priority; GET /api/tasks?assigned_to=alice returns only tasks assigned to that value
Multiple filters combine with AND logic: GET /api/tasks?status=todo&priority=high returns tasks matching both
Unrecognized filter keys and invalid filter values do not cause errors — they are silently ignored and return unfiltered results for that dimension
Tests in tests/filtering.test.ts verify each filter individually, combined filters, and that invalid filter values don't error

stories.title: Pagination with limit and offset
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/tasks accepts ?limit=N&offset=M query parameters; default limit is 20, default offset is 0; limit is capped at 100
Response shape changes to { data: [...], meta: { total: number, limit: number, offset: number, hasMore: boolean } } where total is the count before pagination and hasMore indicates more results exist
Non-numeric or negative limit/offset values fall back to defaults rather than causing errors
Pagination works correctly when combined with filter query parameters from S-004
Tests in tests/pagination.test.ts verify default pagination, custom limit/offset, hasMore flag accuracy, max limit cap, and interaction with filters

stories.title: Sorting tasks by multiple fields
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/tasks accepts ?sort=field&order=asc|desc; allowed sort fields are: created_at, updated_at, priority, title; default is created_at desc
Priority sorts by semantic urgency order (urgent > high > medium > low), not alphabetically
Invalid sort field or order value silently falls back to default sort (created_at desc)
Sorting works correctly when combined with filter and pagination parameters
Tests in tests/sorting.test.ts verify sorting by each field in both directions, priority urgency ordering, and invalid sort fallback

stories.title: API key authentication middleware
acceptanceCriteria: Middleware on all /api/* routes checks for X-API-Key header; valid key (from env var API_KEY, defaulting to 'dev-key-123') allows request through
Missing X-API-Key header returns 401 with { error: 'Unauthorized' }; invalid key value returns 401 with { error: 'Unauthorized' }
GET /health is exempt from authentication and remains publicly accessible
All existing test files for /api/* routes must include X-API-Key header in requests to continue passing
Tests in tests/auth.test.ts verify: request with valid key succeeds, request with missing key returns 401, request with wrong key returns 401, /health without key returns 200

stories.title: Task statistics endpoint
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/stats returns 200 with { data: { total: number, byStatus: { todo: number, in_progress: number, done: number }, byPriority: { low: number, medium: number, high: number, urgent: number }, unassigned: number } }
All counts are accurate and reflect current database state (verified by creating known tasks and asserting counts)
Endpoint requires authentication (X-API-Key header) consistent with S-007
Tests in tests/stats.test.ts create tasks with various status/priority/assignee combinations and verify all counts in the response
